mission.title: Install and Configure vLLM
mission.description: vLLM is a high-throughput and memory-efficient inference and serving engine for large language models (LLMs). It is ideal for applications requiring fast and scalable model serving, especially in environments with diverse hardware. Key trade-offs include the need for GPU resources for optimal performance and potential complexity in configuration.

steps.title: Add Helm Repository
steps.description:
This step adds the vLLM Helm chart repository to your local Helm configuration, allowing you to install vLLM using Helm.

```bash

helm repo add vllm https://github.com/vllm-project/vllm/releases/download/v0.18.1

```

> This adds the specific vLLM version repository.

steps.title: Install Custom Resource Definitions (CRDs)
steps.description:
CRDs are necessary for vLLM to define its custom resources in Kubernetes. This step ensures that all required CRDs are installed.

```bash

kubectl apply -f https://raw.githubusercontent.com/vllm-project/vllm/v0.18.1/deploy/crds/vllm.io_vllms_crd.yaml

```

> This installs the vLLM CRDs.

steps.title: Install vLLM using Helm
steps.description:
This command installs the vLLM application in the specified namespace, allowing you to manage it via Kubernetes.

```bash

helm install vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --create-namespace --version 0.18.1

```

> This installs vLLM in the 'vllm' namespace.

steps.title: Create an Example Custom Resource
steps.description:
This step creates an example vLLM custom resource that defines the model and its configuration for serving.

```bash

kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

```

> This creates a vLLM instance serving the GPT-3 model.
commands:
kubectl apply -f - <<EOF
apiVersion: vllm.io/v1
kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

steps.title: Verify the Custom Resource is Ready
steps.description:
This command checks the status of the vLLM instance to ensure it is running and ready to serve requests.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Look for 'STATUS' to be 'Ready'.

uninstall.title: Uninstall vLLM
uninstall.description:
This step removes the vLLM installation and its associated resources. Be aware that this will delete all data related to the vLLM instance.

```bash

helm uninstall vllm --namespace vllm

kubectl delete namespace vllm

```

> This command uninstalls the vLLM release.
> This command deletes the namespace and all resources within it.

upgrade.title: Upgrade vLLM
upgrade.description:
Before upgrading, ensure you backup your configurations. This step will upgrade vLLM to the latest version and verify the upgrade.

```bash

kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

helm upgrade vllm vllm/vllm --namespace vllm --version 0.18.1

kubectl get vllm -n vllm

```

> Backup current vLLM configuration.
> This command upgrades vLLM to the specified version.
> Verify the vLLM instance is still running after the upgrade.
commands: kubectl get vllm -n vllm -o yaml > vllm-backup.yaml

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'vllm not found'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM custom resource may not be installed or the namespace is incorrect.

**Fix:**
Ensure that the vLLM CRDs are installed and the correct namespace is being used.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Failed to pull image'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The specified container image for vLLM may not exist or is inaccessible.

**Fix:**
Check the image name and ensure it is available in the specified registry.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'Insufficient resources'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The cluster may not have enough resources (CPU/RAM) to schedule the vLLM pods.

**Fix:**
Increase the resources available in your Kubernetes cluster or reduce the resource requests for vLLM.

troubleshooting.title: Error: 'CrashLoopBackOff'
troubleshooting.description:
**Cause:** The vLLM pods may be crashing due to misconfiguration or resource limits.

**Fix:**
Check the pod logs using 'kubectl logs <pod-name>' for more details and adjust configurations accordingly.

versionNotes.changes: Added support for new models and improved memory management features.
versionNotes.migrationSteps: If upgrading from v0.6 or v0.7, ensure to review the new configuration options in the documentation.

resolution.summary: You should see the vLLM instance listed with a status of 'Ready' when running 'kubectl get vllm -n vllm'.

kind: VLLM
metadata:
  name: example-vllm
spec:
  model: 'gpt-3'
  replicas: 2
EOF

prerequisites.description: Ensure you have a Kubernetes cluster with sufficient resources and the necessary tools installed.
